return_convention.geometric_note: Geometric means are auto-derived as: geo ≈ arith − (vol² / 2). Use geometric for deterministic compounding projections; arithmetic for Monte Carlo draws.
return_convention.note: Arithmetic means are typically used for Monte Carlo draws. For deterministic projections, the geometric_return field provides a more conservative long-run compound rate.

reference_inflation.note: Assumed long-run CPI. Nominal returns above already embed this expectation. The app's separate inflation setting should align with or be informed by this figure.

description.purpose: A small, recognisable set of asset buckets that maps well to typical UK defined-contribution (DC) pension fund menus (e.g., Global Equity, Diversified Growth, Bonds/Gilts, Index-Linked, Cash, Property). Use as app defaults, with user override controls.
how_to_refresh_values: Step 1: Pick your target horizon for assumptions (commonly 10-year for planning). Keep it consistent across all asset classes.
Step 2: Choose a reference set (or blend) of institutional Capital Market Assumptions (CMAs) for expected returns and volatilities (e.g., Vanguard/BlackRock/Morningstar house views).
Step 3: Update this JSON's expected_return and volatility for each asset class to match your chosen reference set (or your curated midpoint).
Step 4: geometric_return fields will be auto-recalculated by the app on load — no manual update needed.
Step 5: If you use correlations for portfolio volatility/Monte Carlo, refresh correlations from the same CMA set (or your chosen long-run defaults).
Step 6: Bump version_date and version_tag, and append a changelog entry describing what changed and why.
Step 7: In the UI, display the version_date and an 'Assumptions source' note so users know what they're using.
when_to_refresh: At least annually (common cadence for planning tools).
Whenever interest-rate regimes change materially (bond/cash assumptions can move quickly).
Whenever you intentionally change modelling horizon, inflation view, or currency/hedging policy.
validation_checks: Weights in any user portfolio must sum to 1.0 (within tolerance).
Expected returns should be plausible relative to each other (e.g., cash < bonds < equities over long horizons, typically).
Volatility should be non-negative and broadly consistent (cash lowest; equities highest; diversified growth between).
If correlations are provided: matrix must be symmetric and all values in [-1, +1].
Geometric return must always be ≤ arithmetic return.
description.override_policy: Defaults should be editable per user and per fund/portfolio. Assumptions are not guarantees and should be clearly labelled as planning estimates.

asset_classes.label: Diversified Growth (Multi-Asset)
asset_classes.definition: A blended bucket representing diversified multi-asset / growth / default funds that mix equities, bonds, and diversifiers.

correlations.note: Illustrative long-run placeholders. Use only if you compute portfolio volatility or run correlated Monte Carlo. Refresh from your chosen CMA source if you want tighter accuracy.

global_equity.notes: UK component is price-only (no dividends). US includes dividends. US weighted higher for this reason.

diversified_growth.notes: Synthetic multi-asset proxy: 60% blended equity + 40% gilts

investment_grade_bonds.notes: UK gilt total return (approximate from yield changes)

inflation_linked_bonds.notes: Crude approximation: nominal gilt return minus inflation

cash.notes: UK Bank Rate used as cash return proxy

property.notes: Synthetic REIT-like proxy. No direct UK property data in sources.

changes: Initial release of 6-asset model defaults with optional correlations and common pension-fund label mappings.
Added geometric_return fields derived from arithmetic returns and volatility.
Added risk_score (1-10) per asset class and per portfolio template.
Added portfolio templates: Moderate Multi-Asset (4-asset blend) and Pre-Retirement Cautious.
